gaps.snippet: # [doc = " RFC-0041 §4.2/§9 process-wide arena ceiling this projection reserves against before rendering."] # [doc = " **Declared** — an asserted operational default, not a measured bound: every concurrent"] # [doc = " [`llm_canonical`] call (LSP re-analyses over untrusted editor buffers, per §5 untrusted-input"] # [doc = " coverage) charges the *same* process-global counter"] # [doc = " ([`mycelium_workstack::current_process_bytes`]), so the sum across concurrent renders — not just"] # [doc = " one render's own size — is what this ceiling bounds. Each consuming crate declares its own"] # [doc = " operational default (consumer-side wiring, mirroring the per-invocation `RecursionBudget`"] # [doc = " pattern); a single shared constant is a follow-up centralization item, not introduced silently"] # [doc = " here (`docs/notes/W7-arena-coverage-audit.md`)."] const PROCESS_ARENA_CEILING_BYTES : u64 = 256 * 1024 * 1024 ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const PROCESS_ARENA_CEILING_BYTES` — initializer is not a decidable non-negative integer literal or a known workspace-floor path (e.g. `RecursionBudget::DEFAULT_DEPTH_LIMIT`); no const item production in the grammar, and co-emit refuses to invent a value (ORACLE-R1 A4 / VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Render a closed Core IR [`Node`] as the `LlmCanonical` s-expression surface (RFC-0021 §4.6)."] # [doc = " **Total** over every node kind (enforced by the exhaustive `match`) and **deterministic** (a pure"] # [doc = " function of the node). Preserves the honesty overlay (P2/P3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **RFC-0041 §4.7 W1 (RR-29 guard-hole census):** `Node` is a post-elaboration tree that can exceed"] # [doc = " the L1 parser's 256-frame depth (an editor buffer is untrusted input), and `render_node` recurses"] # [doc = " one stack frame per nesting level. This is the LSP's **outermost public entry point** for the"] # [doc = " render, so it — and only it — wraps the recursion in [`ensure_sufficient_stack`]: a deep buffer"] # [doc = " now renders on the grown worker stack instead of a SIGABRT that would crash the language server."] # [doc = " The budget's depth ceiling is not yet consulted by the W1 host-stack grow (that lands in W2); this"] # [doc = " call site exists so W2's fine-grained guard swaps in with **no signature change** here."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **RFC-0041 §4.2/§9 W7 (process-arena coverage).** This projection allocates a `String` proportional"] # [doc = " to the node count — the process-wide arena hole the §9 DoD names (`docs/notes/W7-arena-coverage-audit.md`"] # [doc = " item 1): an editor buffer is untrusted, and many concurrent LSP re-analyses could otherwise sum past"] # [doc = " any single-pass ceiling. **Signature change (contained):** `llm_canonical` was infallible (`String`)"] # [doc = " before this wave; it is now `Result<String, BudgetError>` so an over-ceiling reservation refuses"] # [doc = " never-silently (G2) instead of proceeding unbounded. This is a **local, contained** change — the"] # [doc = " function is `pub` but not re-exported at the crate root, and every call site is inside this crate"] # [doc = " (tests) — not a ripple into the trusted base (mycelium-interp/mycelium-mlir)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " [`BudgetError::OutOfBudget`] (`kind = Bytes`) when the pre-render reservation would push the"] # [doc = " process-wide arena total over its ceiling."] pub fn llm_canonical (node : & Node) -> Result < String , BudgetError > { let arena = ProcessArena :: new (PROCESS_ARENA_CEILING_BYTES) ; llm_canonical_with_arena (node , & arena) }

A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " [`llm_canonical`], parameterized over an explicit [`ProcessArena`] (`pub(crate)`, so this crate's"] # [doc = " own tests can inject a tiny-ceiling arena and witness a real refusal — production callers always"] # [doc = " go through [`llm_canonical`], which supplies the crate's declared default ceiling)."] pub (crate) fn llm_canonical_with_arena (node : & Node , arena : & ProcessArena ,) -> Result < String , BudgetError > { let budget = RecursionBudget :: with_depth_default (u64 :: MAX , u64 :: MAX) ; ensure_sufficient_stack (& budget , | | { let estimate = node_count (node) . saturating_mul (ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_NODE) ; let _reservation = arena . reserve (estimate) ? ; Ok (render_node (node)) }) }

A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Total node count over the same 11-node grammar `render_node` matches — the pre-flight sizing basis"] # [doc = " for the arena reservation above. `Exact` (a deterministic structural count); the *bytes-per-node*"] # [doc = " multiplier applied to it is the `Declared` part, not this count itself."] fn node_count (n : & Node) -> u64 { 1 + match n { Node :: Const (_) | Node :: Var (_) => 0 , Node :: Let { bound , body , .. } => node_count (bound) + node_count (body) , Node :: Op { args , .. } | Node :: Construct { args , .. } => { args . iter () . map (node_count) . sum :: < u64 > () } Node :: Swap { src , .. } => node_count (src) , Node :: Match { scrutinee , alts , default , } => { node_count (scrutinee) + alts . iter () . map (alt_node_count) . sum :: < u64 > () + default . as_deref () . map_or (0 , node_count) } Node :: Lam { body , .. } | Node :: Fix { body , .. } => node_count (body) , Node :: App { func , arg } => node_count (func) + node_count (arg) , Node :: FixGroup { defs , body } => { defs . iter () . map (| (_ , d) | node_count (d)) . sum :: < u64 > () + node_count (body) } } }

gaps.snippet: fn alt_node_count (a : & Alt) -> u64 { match a { Alt :: Ctor { body , .. } | Alt :: Lit { body , .. } => node_count (body) , } }

gaps.snippet: fn render_node (n : & Node) -> String { match n { Node :: Const (v) => format ! ("(const {})" , render_value (v)) , Node :: Var (x) => x . clone () , Node :: Let { id , bound , body } => { format ! ("(let [{id} {}] {})" , render_node (bound) , render_node (body)) } Node :: Op { prim , args } => format ! ("(op {prim}{})" , render_args (args)) , Node :: Swap { src , target , policy , } => format ! ("(swap! {} :to {} :policy {})" , render_node (src) , render_repr (target) , policy . as_str ()) , Node :: Construct { ctor , args } => format ! ("(make {ctor}{})" , render_args (args)) , Node :: Match { scrutinee , alts , default , } => render_match (scrutinee , alts , default . as_deref ()) , Node :: Lam { param , body } => format ! ("(fn [{param}] {})" , render_node (body)) , Node :: App { func , arg } => format ! ("({} {})" , render_node (func) , render_node (arg)) , Node :: Fix { name , body } => format ! ("(fix {name} {})" , render_node (body)) , Node :: FixGroup { defs , body } => { let binds : String = defs . iter () . map (| (name , def) | format ! ("[{name} {}]" , render_node (def))) . collect :: < Vec < _ > > () . join (" ") ; format ! ("(fix-group ({binds}) {})" , render_node (body)) } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Space-prefixed operand list, e.g. ` a b c` (empty for no args)."] fn render_args (args : & [Node]) -> String { args . iter () . map (| a | format ! (" {}" , render_node (a))) . collect () }

gaps.snippet: fn render_match (scrutinee : & Node , alts : & [Alt] , default : Option < & Node >) -> String { let mut arms : Vec < String > = alts . iter () . map (render_alt) . collect () ; if let Some (d) = default { arms . push (format ! ("(_ {})" , render_node (d))) ; } format ! ("(match {} {})" , render_node (scrutinee) , arms . join (" ")) }

gaps.snippet: fn render_alt (alt : & Alt) -> String { match alt { Alt :: Ctor { ctor , binders , body , } => { let pat = if binders . is_empty () { format ! ("{ctor}") } else { format ! ("({ctor} {})" , binders . join (" ")) } ; format ! ("({pat} {})" , render_node (body)) } Alt :: Lit { value , body } => format ! ("({} {})" , render_value (value) , render_node (body)) , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Render a constant value: its literal (repr + payload) plus the honesty overlay — the guarantee tag"] # [doc = " always (P2), and bound presence when approximate (P6-adjacent)."] fn render_value (v : & Value) -> String { let lit = render_payload (v . repr () , v . payload ()) ; let guar = guar_str (v . meta () . guarantee ()) ; let bound = if v . meta () . bound () . is_some () { " :bound" } else { "" } ; format ! ("{lit} @{guar}{bound}") }

gaps.snippet: fn render_payload (repr : & Repr , payload : & Payload) -> String { match (repr , payload) { (Repr :: Binary { .. } , Payload :: Bits (bits)) => { if bits . len () > INLINE_MAX { format ! ("0b<{} bits>" , bits . len ()) } else { let s : String = bits . iter () . map (| & b | if b { '1' } else { '0' }) . collect () ; format ! ("0b{s}") } } (Repr :: Ternary { .. } , Payload :: Trits (trits)) => { if trits . len () > INLINE_MAX { format ! ("<{} trits>" , trits . len ()) } else { let s : String = trits . iter () . map (| t | match t { Trit :: Neg => '-' , Trit :: Zero => '0' , Trit :: Pos => '+' , }) . collect () ; format ! ("<{s}>") } } (Repr :: Dense { dtype , .. } , Payload :: Scalars (xs)) => { let s : String = xs . iter () . map (| x | format ! ("{x}")) . collect :: < Vec < _ > > () . join (" ") ; format ! ("[{s}]:{}" , scalar_str (* dtype)) } (Repr :: Vsa { model , sparsity , .. } , Payload :: Hypervector (xs) ,) => { format ! ("<hv:{model}/{}{}>" , xs . len () , sparsity_str (sparsity)) } (r , _) => format ! ("<malformed-value:{}>" , render_repr (r)) , } }

gaps.snippet: fn render_repr (r : & Repr) -> String { match r { Repr :: Binary { width } => format ! ("Binary{{{width}}}") , Repr :: Ternary { trits } => format !
